Why It Feels AI-Generic: When headline, cards, badges, and CTAs all shout, nothing leads the eye.
Fix: Pick one dominant headline and one supporting proof rhythm; reduce visual volume elsewhere.
Keywords: generic ai ui hierarchy noisy bento loud

Why It Feels AI-Generic: Spraying gradients across cards, buttons, sections, and backgrounds reads as fake polish.
Fix: Keep gradients to one hero moment or one accent surface; let neutrals do the rest.

Why It Feels AI-Generic: When one card is 6px, another is 20px, one has soft shadow, another none, the page feels stitched together.
Fix: Lock radius scale and shadow scale before styling components.

Why It Feels AI-Generic: Phrases like revolutionize, seamless, next-generation without proof make the UI feel template-made.
column6: and product proof near the first CTA.

Why It Feels AI-Generic: Bento grids look modern, but if every tile has equal weight the section becomes pretty noise.
If multiple things float, glow, slide, and pulse at once, the UI starts feeling fake.
Fix: Animate only state changes and 1-2 signature moments per view.

Why It Feels AI-Generic: Different button colors, fills, outlines, glows, and sizes weaken decision clarity.
Fix: Use one primary CTA style and one secondary style for most screens.
Keywords: too many cta styles button inconsistency

Why It Feels AI-Generic: Breaking every idea into a card creates clutter and kills narrative flow.
Fix: Use cards only for things that need grouping or interaction; let sections breathe otherwise.

Smell: Style copied at surface level only
Why It Feels AI-Generic: Copying black background or blur without the spacing and typography DNA results in a weak imitation.
Thin low-contrast text on tinted glass might screenshot well but fails real use.
column6: and test body text at normal reading distance.
